title: How Digital Creators in Tanzania Sell Products Online: Which Option Works Regardless of Where You Are
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: Creators in Nigeria, Pakistan, Ghana, Bangladesh, and dozens of other countries cannot use the platforms everyone else takes for granted. This changes that.

content.text: As a digital creator in Tanzania, you've likely experienced the frustration of having a great product to sell, but being blocked from collecting payment due to geographic restrictions. You're not alone, as many creators in Africa and other developing countries face the same challenge. With a growing demand for digital products, it's essential to find a reliable and accessible way to sell and receive payments online, regardless of where you are in the world.
What You Are Actually Comparing

When considering options for selling digital products online, you're likely comparing traditional platforms like PayPal, Stripe, and Gumroad, which promise easy payment processing and global reach. However, for creators in Tanzania and other restricted countries, these platforms often fall short due to strict KYC requirements, limited payment options, and high transaction fees. It's essential to look beyond these traditional platforms and explore alternative solutions that cater to your specific needs.
Where the Traditional Platforms Break Down

Traditional platforms like PayPal and Stripe often break down for creators in Tanzania due to their strict requirements and limited support for local payment methods. For instance, PayPal may not be available in your country, or Stripe may require a US or European bank account, which can be difficult to obtain. Additionally, these platforms often charge high transaction fees, eating into your profit margins and making it harder to sustain your business.
What Crypto-Native Infrastructure Does Differently

Crypto-native infrastructure, on the other hand, offers a more accessible and reliable solution for creators in Tanzania. By leveraging blockchain technology and cryptocurrencies like Bitcoin and Ethereum, you can receive payments from anywhere in the world, without the need for traditional banking systems or intermediaries. This approach also eliminates the need for KYC requirements, making it easier to get started and sell your products globally.
The ability to receive payments from anywhere in the world, without restrictions or intermediaries, is a game-changer for digital creators in Tanzania and other developing countries.
The Real Cost Difference

When considering the cost of using traditional platforms versus crypto-native infrastructure, it's essential to look beyond the initial fees and consider the long-term implications. Traditional platforms may charge lower fees upfront, but their restrictions and limitations can lead to lost sales and revenue. In contrast, crypto-native infrastructure may require a one-time payment for setup, but it offers a more sustainable and scalable solution, with lower transaction fees and greater control over your sales and revenue.
Which One to Use and Why

For creators in Tanzania and other restricted countries, crypto-native infrastructure is often the better choice due to its accessibility, reliability, and lower transaction fees. By using a platform like Unchained Commerce, you can set up a digital store with automated delivery, payment verification, and real-time analytics, without the need for traditional banking systems or intermediaries. This approach also offers greater control over your sales and revenue, making it easier to sustain and grow your business.
What Creators in Restricted Countries Are Asking

As a digital creator in Tanzania, you can sell your products globally without geographic restrictions by using crypto-native infrastructure, which offers a more accessible and reliable solution. You can receive international payments for your digital products, regardless of where your customers are located, and without the need for traditional banking systems or intermediaries. This approach also eliminates the need for KYC requirements, making it easier to get started and sell your products online, even if you're in a country that Stripe or PayPal doesn't support.
Crypto-native infrastructure offers a more accessible and reliable solution for creators, with lower transaction fees and greater control over their sales and revenue.
Related Topics Worth Reading

Selling ebooks from Africa using crypto, or receiving international payments as a creator in Pakistan, are just a few examples of the many use cases for crypto-native infrastructure. By leveraging blockchain technology and cryptocurrencies, you can access a global market and sell your digital products to anyone, anywhere in the world, without the restrictions and limitations of traditional platforms.
